subject: Human Resources
title: What Organizational Culture Really Is
description: One-minute skill: What Organizational Culture Really Is.

hook.text: 'Culture eats strategy for breakfast.' Drucker reportedly said it—decades later, CEOs quote it without changing culture.

buildup.text: Schein defined culture on three levels: artifacts (visible), espoused values (stated), and basic assumptions (unconscious).

discovery.text: The real culture lives at the deepest level—the unwritten rules about how things actually work, not the posters on the wall.

twist.text: Companies claim 'innovation' as a value but punish failure. The stated culture and real culture are often completely different.

climax.text: Changing culture means changing assumptions—what gets rewarded, tolerated, and punished.

punchline.text: Culture isn't what you say you value. It's what you actually tolerate.

quiz.question: According to Schein, where does real culture live?
options: In basic assumptions—unconscious beliefs about how things work
In company posters and mission statements
